blurb: The Centre for Future Generations (CFG), a European (EU-focused) think tank on the governance of emerging technologies including advanced AI, biotech and neurotech.

publications.title: What Europeans think about solar radiation modification
publications.summary: Surveys European public awareness and attitudes toward solar radiation modification as the technology rises up the science and policy agenda.

publications.title: Enforcement spotlight – Spring 2026
publications.summary: Assesses where the EU's digital rulebook stands as AI stress-tests its limits, comparing four regulatory frameworks responding to the same technology.

publications.title: Towards an EU Neurotechnology Strategy
publications.summary: White paper synthesising two years of CFG neurotech research into policy recommendations, calling for European action on neurotechnology governance.

news.title: Press Release: the RAISE trilemma
news.summary: CFG critiques the EU's flagship 'CERN for AI' (RAISE) ambition, warning that without focus Europe's initiative could end up 'everything and nothing at once' — a sharp EU-AI-industrial-policy intervention.

news.title: CFG Board appoints Thomas Lingard as CEO
news.summary: CFG names Thomas Lingard CEO (effective March 2025), bringing 25+ years in sustainability, innovation and policy — a leadership signal for the future-generations think tank.

notes: cfg.eu is Cloudflare-gated for direct curl page-body reads (Attention Required), and /publications/ 403s to curl. Sitemap XML (cfg.eu/post-sitemap.xml) fetched fine via curl; titles/dates/summaries confirmed via web_fetch of /publications/ which rendered the publications index. Dates are CFG's own publication dates (DD.MM.YYYY on the index). No first-party press-release feed distinct from publications; news empty. No legal/regulatory action against CFG found; controversies empty.
